entry.title: SAP Field Service Management 2411 - Available Now!
entry.content:
SAP Field Service Management 2411 builds on our strategic investment in generative AI for field service organizations, now with extended multilingual AI support. In addition, we introduced several new features designed to simplify user workflows, strengthen integration, and increase functionality. In this blog, you can find an overview of the most notable features, along with a demo video showcasing key highlights. For a complete list of all the features in the 2411 release, click here. In this blog, I will highlight the key features that will help enhance how you manage field services, including:

  • Generative AI: Multilingual support (French and German) for the Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering.
  • User Experience enhancements: New Morning Horizon theme, Business Partner enhancements, manage work time in the Time & Material Journal, extended sort and filter capabilities, extended customization capabilities, and more.
  • Service Map: Visualize activity details on hover, and a simplified view of travel time/distance.
  • Integration: Enhanced SAP S/4HANA Service integration, New Fiori App for viewing SAP FSM activities, Production Order integration, and item-based accounting for an unplanned service.
  • AI-based scheduling improvements: Simulate the effect of adjustments in auto-scheduling policies, and recalculate travel time for bookings.

Note: The Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering features require a customer to be licensed for SAP AI Units. For more information about licensing, please contact your SAP Account Executive. 

Generative AI

SAP Field Service Management Generative AI services are now available in French and German, enabling more users to access Gen AI features in their native language. In addition to simplifying workflows, generative AI in SAP Field Service Management makes it easier to unlock and benefit from valuable insights that were previously difficult to access. These enhanced workflows and actionable insights provide substantial value to field service organizations. According to SAP customer benchmarking, by utilizing these features, field service organizations can achieve (on average):

  • ⬆️ 12.5% increase in dispatcher productivity.
  • ⬆️ 5% improvement in first-time fix rates.
  • ⬇️ 15% reduction in time to review and plan service requests.

Take a look at the demo video below showcasing our AI highlights in 2411:


AI Assisted Services - Now Available in German and French

AI Assisted Services (German 🇩🇪)

AI Services in German - Intelligent Filtering and Activity Summary

 
AI Assisted Services (French 🇫🇷) 

AI Services in French - Intelligent Filtering and Activity Summary
➡️ For more information on AI services such as Intelligent Filtering, Equipment Insights, and the Activity Summary, please refer to the 2408 Release Blog. These translations are made possible using SAP Translation Hub.

AI-based Scheduling Improvements:

Simulate the Effect of Adjustments in Custom Auto-scheduling Policies

The new Policy Simulation feature allows users to test scheduling policy changes in a controlled environment, ensuring no impact on live schedules. This enhancement allows users to configure and evaluate adjustments, such as prioritizing skills levels or enforcing objectives, in a secure environment. By simulating outcomes, organizations can safely refine strategies, and ensure alignment with evolving operating conditions. Learn more about this innovation here.

Recalculate Travel Time for Bookings

Thanks to feedback from our Customer Influence Program, accurate travel times are now easier to achieve via automatic travel time recalculation. Travel times are updated automatically when planning or replanning activities, ensuring dispatchers always have precise data. This enhancement helps reduce manual adjustments, minimizes dispatcher confusion, and improves overall scheduling accuracy. Learn more about this feature here.


User Experience & Usability Enhancements

SAP Field Service Management 2411 also features several key user experience and usability highlights, which are summarized below and in the demo video:


Morning Horizon Theme

The new Morning Horizon theme in SAP Field Service Management introduces a fresh, modern design, providing users with a consistent and visually appealing interface. This standard theme enhances the user experience with improved aesthetics, accessibility, and a cohesive look and feel across all SAP applications. Learn more here.

 

SAP Feedback Survey

This provides an easy way to contribute feedback on product functionality, helping SAP Field Service Management refine and enhance its offerings based on real-world usage and customer needs. Learn more about participating here.


Dispatching Board

Configurable Header Information in the Modal UI (preview)

The new header configuration in SAP Field Service Management allows organizations to customize the key details displayed in the Modal UI for Service Calls and Activities via screen configurations. This enhancement makes it easier to display critical information by tailoring screens to meet your company’s unique requirements. Learn more about this feature here.



Manage Approvals for Worktime in the Time and Material Journal

The enhanced work time management capabilities in SAP Field Service Management improve tracking and approval of time entries through the Time and Material (T&M) Journal. This update harmonizes processes in the T&M Journal and improves workflows for work time approvals. Learn more about this feature here.

 
Option to Display the Activity Cancellation Reason 

Users can now benefit from increased transparency for cancelled activities in SAP Field Service Management, with clear visibility into the reasons for activity cancellations. For more information, visit the product documentation.


Service Call Extension Tab in the Modal UI

Extensions in SAP Field Service Management now provide greater flexibility for placement in desired outlets. The new Service Call extension tab in the Modal UI allows users to position extensions in more key locations, ensuring they are easily accessible and aligned with operational priorities. For more details, click here.

Sample Extension

 
Option to Save Column Layouts in all List Views (Preview)

The 2411 release provides enhanced flexibility for customizing column settings and layouts of the activity list. Users can easily configure visible fields and group data to align with specific operational needs and tactical scheduling scenarios, creating a more intuitive user experience with faster access to relevant data. For further info, see here.

 
Enhancements for List Views

This release introduces improved filtering capabilities, offering enhanced options for managing list views. Users can now easily filter by key information when working with a list view in the planning list, making this workflow faster and more efficient. More information, here.


Service Map

Quick Access to Activity Details

Dispatchers can now view essential activity information from the Service Map by simply hovering over an activity, reducing the number of clicks. This popup allows dispatchers to instantly view key details, and can be easily configured to display the companies specific requirements. For more details, please see here.


Service Map – Close/Dock Time-Distance Popup Box

When using the Service Map, dispatchers now can easily view detailed travel information directly on the Service Map with a simple pop-up box. This pop-up displays both travel time and distance, and can be opened, moved, and closed as needed, allowing dispatchers to quickly access and manage information without cluttering the screen. More details here.


Integration Improvements

New Fiori App for Viewing SAP Field Service Management Activities

The new SAP S/4HANA Fiori App for viewing SAP FSM activities provides a simplified and intuitive way to access activity details from SAP Field Service Management. With an organized data presentation, users can quickly navigate through activities and view critical information such as status, technician assignments, and partner data, directly from SAP S/4HANA. Get the full details here.


Cancellation of Activity from SAP S/4HANA

In SAP S/4HANA, SAP FSM activities can now be cancelled with detailed reasons, while seamlessly integrated into SAP Field Service Management. This ensures consistent data and improved transparency across both systems. For more details, refer to the help documentation.


Item-based Accounting for Unplanned Service

Item-based accounting and revenue recognition in SAP S/4HANA Service now integrates with SAP Field Service Management to support unplanned services. This enhancement ensures that revenue is accurately tracked and recognized based on actual service execution, bridging financial, profitability, and operational data for improved transparency and compliance. See the support documentation here.


Integration of Production Orders as Service Calls

Production Order integration with SAP Field Service Management enhances the connection between SAP Field Service Management and SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC. This integration enables scheduling, execution, and confirmation of production order operations, with support for time effort, materials management, and more. More information here.

Final Thoughts

SAP Field Service Management 2411 enhances the field service value chain with multilingual generative AI, streamlined workflows, and improved integration capabilities. We invite you to explore these new capabilities, share your feedback, and help us shape the future of SAP Field Service Management by submitting your influence requests here. Stay tuned for the 2502 release, where even more innovations are on the way!

Get in touch!

Thoughts or questions? Feel free to share any feedback in a comment. It would also be great to hear directly from you which AI innovations you think could be the most impactful for future releases. For related posts and more information, please join our SAP Field Service Management Community page and explore our SAP Field Service Management Website. You can also watch the SAP Field Service Management 2411 Release Demo here, and I encourage you to register for our upcoming webinars for the most up to date information by registering here.

entry.title: How KTern.AI is leveraging SAP AI to take their SAP store industry solution to the next level
entry.content:
As SAP moves to include “AI everywhere” and offering platforms and tools like SAP BTP Cloud Foundry environment to the market, it is great to see SAP partners enhancing their solutions with these AI capabilities. 

I have earlier written about the KTern.AI solution, an approved industry cloud solution for the professional services industry which helps customers and implementers collaborate on digital transformation projects like SAP RISE projects. 

KTern.AI has now taken the next step with their solution and included SAP AI platform to accelerate moving custom code from an SAP on-premises world to a “keeping the code clean” world on SAP BTP.  This is done by leveraging special Large Language Models to automate the process.   Read more below.  

Interview with Ratnakumar Nagarajan, Executive Director @ Kaar, Chief Product Officer @ KTern.AI.

Question:  What does your solution do? 
Answer: KTern.AI automation platform accelerates RISE with SAP centric digital transformations with AI-First approach. Over the last 2 decades, enterprise customers have piled up their legacy customizations, leading to longer upgrade cycles, higher maintenance costs and performance inefficiencies. As a pioneering solution, KTern.AI drives clean core modernization with SAP Business AI powered custom code mining in SAP ERP core, optimization & transformation into SAP BTP extensions. It helps enterprises achieve faster innovation cycles, minimal disruptions, improved agility, and long-term cost savings.

Q: How does it work?
A: To drive SAP clean core automation, KTern.AI works with a data-driven, non-invasive, AI-First approach. It connects to the SAP ERP system through standard SAP RFC protocol, scans the data in the SAP system including the as-is custom codes. It performs to-be segmentation into side-by-side SAP BTP extensions and in-app extensions, based on materiality and complexity. To build business apps, KTern.AI offers its inbuilt CodeGenie buildspaces to simulate and generate LLM powered code built on RESTful ABAP Application Programming (RAP) & Cloud Application Programming (CAP) models, with industry best practices. The end-to-end automation built on SAP AI Core powered AWS Bedrock foundational models, accelerates transition to a clean core architecture, ensuring cloud readiness while minimizing manual intervention and reducing technical debt.

Q: How can the system manage to create new code?
KTern.AI generates new code using a specially trained Large Language Model (LLM) designed for SAP environments tapping on SAP Generative AI Hub. This LLM understands the intricacies of SAP’s customizations and generates optimized, cloud-compatible code. The key advantage lies in KTern.AI Hanapedia – SAP Tribal Knowledge Base engine, with reinforced learning capabilities, built on deep industry knowledge from our 15+ years of SAP excellence. It enhances the LLM’s ability to generate relevant & accurate solutions for specific business needs, based on existing custom codes or Functional Requirement Specifications (FRS) document fed as input. Additionally, KTern.AI’s value comes from its automated workflows, intelligent decision-making insights on the overall system landscape, which streamlines the transition and ensure code transformation with a clean core strategy.

Q: How much effort does the customer / partner need to put into checking the outcome?
A: In our experience with Oil & Gas based industry in the US, KTern.AI automated upto 60% of the extensibility assessment and code generation tasks for custom code during their SAP S/4HANA transformation with clean core approach. The platform identified legacy customizations and provided actionable insights, helping customer and system integrator to review and take the final data-driven decisions. Our AI-driven approach ensured accuracy and efficiency, allowing the customer to focus more on strategic decision-making rather than manually interpreting code analysis results, as part of their RISE with SAP led Business Transformation Program.

Q: Which tools and technologies do the solution leverage?
KTern.AI leverages SAP BTP fueled SAP AI Core & Generative AI Hub, that act as the cornerstone of clean core automation. The solution uses proprietary Large Language Models (LLMs) tailored for SAP custom code analysis and transformation into modular applications, ensuring high accuracy in identifying and modernizing legacy customizations. The KTern.AI platform utilizes the SAP AI Launchpad to access AI scenarios, and SAP AI Core to provide an engine to AWS Bedrock foundational models. The SAP AI Core Systems help build the CI/CD pipeline and synchronization across the DevOps & MLOps lifecycle, thanks to the integration with SAP Cloud ALM. With the intelligent tech driven by SAP Business AI, the KTern.AI platform helps you achieve AI-first modernization with code transformation and modularized SAP BTP extensions for increased business productivity and faster innovation cycles.

Q: Can you specify about how you leverage SAP AI Core capabilities and services?
KTern.AI utilizes SAP AI Core service enabled in SAP BTP Cloud Foundry environment for high accuracy and efficiency in outputs. The Generative AI Hub powered AWS Bedrock Claude 3.5 Sonnet model helps perform triangulated complex study of business requirements, custom codes and tribal knowledge base, to generate codes based on RAP & CAP models and build business apps at ease. Thanks to prompt engineering & management capabilities, the Generative AI Hub is the backbone of KTern.AI DevZone – an intelligent, cutting-edge IDE with hybrid intelligence and intuitive data model visualizations.  By using SAP AI Core’s scalable compute resources, KTern.AI efficiently processes large volumes of custom code and drives automation with high accuracy for faster clean core transformations towards a Composable Enterprise. KTern.AI is listed on the SAP Discovery Center, to help SAP customers & partners get onboarded with SAP AI Core services for the  clean core mission.

Q: What is your vision and plans for the solution moving forward?
Our vision is to democratize SAP centric digital transformations with autonomous intelligence. In our pursuit to drive continuous modernization for enterprises with clean core strategy and empower system integrators, KTern.AI intends to deploy autonomous bots with advanced intelligence, to continuously assess, adapt, optimize business applications. Our collaboration with SAP Co-Innovation Labs (COIL) on Generative AI intends to empower 5M+ developers, increase productivity of 300M+ cloud users across 1000+ partners and 40,000+ enterprises in SAP ecosystem. As KTern.AI platform offers the SAP Business AI driven value realization with right use case, it addresses the right tech needs of SAP-run enterprises, that puts business innovation and agility at its core. With 7x times faster SAP innovation cycles and 30% reduced efforts in SAP BTP implementations at the moment, we intend to continuously improve the business outcomes and drive customer success, through Digital Clean Core as a Service (DCaaS) delivery automation model powered by KTern.AI.

Conclusion:
There are so many great aspects of this story which support SAP strategies: leveraging GEN AI to automate business processes, built on SAP BTP AI capabilities, making SAP RISE and other transformations more efficient, and partner-to-partner collaboration for partner ecosystem efficiency!

If you are an organisation running SAP and embarking on a digital transformation journey, consider contacting KTern.AI to leverage their solution.  If you are an SAP partner running SAP RISE projects, consider doing the same.

 Link to KTern.AI on SAP store

More about Kaar:
KTern.AI is an Independent Product division of Kaar Technologies - A global IT firm focused on providing SAP services like SAP ERP Implementation, SAP Upgrade, SAP Rollout, SAP module implementation and SAP Support Services for different verticals in the industry like Oil & Gas, Petrochemicals, Manufacturing, Public Sectors, Engineering & Construction, Transportation, Education, Healthcare, Media and FMCG. With a strong base of 2500 + SAP associates and through many successful ventures and partnerships, Kaar Technologies has completed over 1000 + SAP projects and worked with many reputed organizations world over. KTern.AI is a key pillar in this journey as a 'Digital Transformation as a Service' (DXaaS) automation delivery model which reduces 24% of the effort with hyper automation tapping our tribal knowledge base having two decade years of industry experience

entry.title: Where can I learn how to prepare and run a Business AI Explore Workshop?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Applying a Human-Centered Approach to Identify and Define Business AI Use Cases learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey explains in detail how to run two interactive sessions aimed at exploring and defining AI use cases based on business and user needs. 
Challenge: Spot the Differences and Win!
Throughout the lessons of this training, our crafty speakers have made some sneaky changes in the set. Can you catch them?
Your Mission: Watch closely all videos and note differences in the set between videos from different lessons (e.g. different plant between lesson 1 and 3). Note as many as possible and send your list to apphaus@sap.com.
What's in it for you?
The first 10 participants to send 20 differences will receive a printed set of the Opportunity, Limitation, and Ideation cards - perfect for your Business AI Explore and Design workshops!

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Prepare and run a Business AI Explore Workshop with strategic decision makers to ideate and identify AI use cases with business value.
  • Prepare and run a Business AI Design workshop with business and IT experts to understand user requirements for a use case idea or existing process, ideate valuable AI capabilities to improve it, and define a future user experience enriched with AI.

 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP Business AI: Crossing the Chasm
entry.content:
It was a little more than a year ago I penned the blog "Why SAP Joule is a different attempt this time?". Fast forward to today, it's evident that Joule is the most significant AI offering from SAP in recent years.

On one hand, it was all good and great with waves of SAP Business AI capabilities showcased in SAP SAPPHIRE and TechEd. On the other hand, there is a growing murmur of "AI Fatigue" among customers. Amidst the "AI Hype", where do we stand with the SAP Business AI adoption now?

If we use Gartner's "Hype Cycle", through this year, the "AI Hype" has likely passed the "Peak of Inflated Expectation" and is edging towards "Disillusionment". What does it mean for the Business AI adoption as a new technology? When the Hype Cycle maps to the "Diffusion of Innovations" (from Everett Rogers): instead of a smooth transition, there is a "Chasm" (by Geoffrey A. Moore) emerges between the "Early Adopters" to the "Early Majority". What is a Chasm? It is a critical gap or barrier that many high-tech products face when transitioning from the early adopters (enthusiastic, risk-tolerant consumers) to the early majority (more pragmatic, risk-averse consumers) in the Technology Adoption Life Cycle.

Diagram 1: Hype Cycle and Technology Adoption Lifecycle Plotted Together

SAP Joule and the Business AI are gaining traction among "Early Adopters", but how can they bridge the chasm to reach the "Early Majority"? In the book "Crossing the Chasm", Moore offers several recommendations, to put them in the context of SAP Business AI:

  • Comprehensive Coverage of Capabilities: This may be the natural one and SAP is diligently expanding the AI offerings with more out-of-box AI capabilities and also the extensions available. This mirrors the evolution of Fiori (firstly introduced about a decade ago!), customers had challenges selecting from the limited Fiori app library to nowadays "Fiori as default".

  • Building Credibility Through Success Stories: This is the more challenging one, why? Because there is a long existing stereotype that by the "Pace Layering" framework, SAP is often viewed as the "System of Records" which is with infrequent changes and need stability. That is correct in the context of traditional Business Suite era: 5+ years between a major upgrade was normal practice. The On-Premise solution release cycle was counted by years, with the custom codes as technical debt, it became very difficult to make a business case to justify a million-dollar upgrade even yearly, not to mention in months.

Diagram 2: Pace Layering Framework

 

Things started to change with the Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) competitors occurring in the “Differentiation” layer with Line of Business (LoB) specific solutions in the Sales, then Finance and Human Resources eroding the Business Suite market. Additionally, the large Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) providers disrupted the software development, deployment and operations. In response, SAP acquired LoB SaaS solution providers and then SAP introduced HANA Cloud Platform which evolved into todays “Business Technology Platform” (BTP) to tackle the “innovation” tier.

However, introducing new technologies is easier than changing perceptions. For SAP Business AI adoption, raising awareness is crucial. As Enterprise Architects, we’ve experimented with various approaches, and I found the following one worked relatively well:

Diagram 3: AI Adoption Approach with SAP Premium Engagement

 

  • "Myth Buster" Session (1 hour): Supported by the SAP AI Centre of Expertise, this session will provide the basic knowledge of the SAP AI capabilities and a high-level overview on the potentially relevant use cases.

  • Architect Point of View (aPoV) Workshops (2 days): For customers seeking deeper understanding and live demos, these workshops offer a holistic view - from embedded AI (e.g. Machine Learning) to the latest Generative AI capabilities. They include live demos and preliminary use case discussions. Feedback indicates that customers are often surprised by the available capabilities and their ease of application.

  • Identifying AI Use Cases: This can be challenging due to mismatched expectations and technological realities. The nuances sometimes can be road blockers afterwards. The tools like SAP Signavio can provide scenario recommendations based on the systems statistics. In this part we advise customers not only thinking of a high-level story but also break down the use cases into Business View, Technical View and Ethical View, utilising templates from SAP AppHaus like below.

Table 1: AI Scenario Formulation Sentence by SAP AppHaus

 

  • Applying Out-of-box AI capabilities: There are Machine Learning or GenAI capabilities ready to use from either S/4HANA or other SAP solutions. The reality is often less straightforward. Customers frequently face blockers because of the lower S/4HANA version or the absence of a required component in the SaaS setup, e.g. Joule in SAP SuccessFactors needs Employee Center. While these challenges are common, Clean Core principles and supporting practices help customers overcome such blockers, enabling them to modernise systems and keep pace with innovation - recognising that cloud transformation is a journey, not a quick fix (see another blog: A Case Study: Modernise the Development Practice with Clean Core Principles).

  • Starting Small with MVPs: Some use cases need to be built from scratch and beginning with “short and sweet” MVPs is the key. This approach focuses on verifying technological feasibility, like using Large Language Models (LLM) or Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in Proof-of-Concepts (PoCs). The emphasis should be on agility and iterative progress rather than aiming for perfection in the first attempt.

  • Setting Proper Expectations and Adoption Models: Properly managing end-user expectations is critical to avoid disillusionment. Because AI models, even when fine-tuned based on user input, may lack flexibility if overfitted to specific data. Establishing a continuous feedback loop ensures models stay relevant and adaptable to the latest data and changing business needs.

In summary, enterprises are increasingly adopting AI in the business solutions. The hype from the personal AI use scenarios created the illusion that "AI will also solve every business problem", leading to frustrations and challenges for the expansion of adoption. While SAP has doubled down in AI for the business solutions, it will take time simply because the complexity, integration and ethical considerations involved. Zooming out, we see SAP striving to cross the chasm of AI adoption. With the task forces from the SAP engineering, delivery and partners exploring different approaches, the "best practices" are emerging. As momentum builds, the chasm will be crossed, and Business AI will become a standard component of "Business As Usual".

 

Diagram 1 reference: https://aesanetwork.org/beyond-rogers-diffusion-of-innovations-and-extension-curricula/

Diagram 2 reference: https://docs-eam.leanix.net/docs/pace-layering

entry.title: Antrhopic released a protocol for AI Agents and Tools based on a Client/Server architecture
entry.content:
ODBC, HTTP, or SOA, but for AI. And brought to you by the #1 model for Instruction following and Tool Calling provider.

Anthropic releases a toolkit for connecting large language models to external systems, they have called it Model Context Protocol, or MCP for short, and is available under an open-source license.

The problem they are trying to solve

No standard way of connecting data sources to models exists just yet; this decision is left to enterprise users and model and database providers.

Today, developers write a specific Python code in LangChain or LlamaIndex, pointing LLMs to databases. With each LLM functioning a little differently from each other, developers need a separate code for each one to connect to specific data sources. This often results in different models calling to the same databases without the ability to work together seamlessly. 

 

The protocol aims to eliminate the need for developers to write redundant custom integration code every time they need to link a new tool or data source to an AI system. Instead, MCP provides a unified method for all these connections, allowing developers to spend more time building features and less time on integration. To illustrate MCP’s broad utility, imagine AI models needing to interact with different types of data: from PostgreSQL databases to cloud platforms like Google Drive or Slack. MCP’s goal is to streamline these interactions, reducing the manual coding that’s currently necessary.

The interaction between AI agents and web services is becoming increasingly complex, as current methods like web scraping and simulated browser interactions have significant challenges. Web scraping suffers from issues such as inconsistent data extraction due to changing HTML structures and potential legal and ethical violations, while simulated browser interactions are resource-intensive and struggle with dynamic content.

Integration is also really comlplicated due to a lack of standardization in APIs and data formats.

 

Anthropic wants to solve this, instead offering a built-in search or similar tool, Anthropic brings an open-source protocol. This protocol it’s not just for Anthropic’s models, but for any model, enabling a plug-and-play ecosystem where tools and LLMs can be swapped with ease.

The MCP works as a two-way bridge between data sources and tools. For now, the Claude desktop app acts as the host, but it’s easy to imagine integrations expanding to platforms like VS Code or other productivity tools. The protocol lets the LLM fetch data from external sources and even send back outputs, opening the door to a multitude of use cases from weather queries to dynamic code editing directly in an IDE such as Cursor or VS Code.

Anthropic provides array of pre-built MCP servers Anthropic offers, covering GitHub integrations, file systems, scraping tools, and others. They’ve also provided SDKs in TypeScript and Python, empowering developers to create custom servers tailored to specific needs. The protocol currently focuses on local integrations through stdio, though there are discussions about supporting additional transport methods like SSE and WebSockets. 

This means that initially, MCP focuses on local connections, meaning the server runs on the same machine as the client.

The concept of LLMs using external protocols isn’t new. Take ChatGPT plugins from March 2023, which aimed to enable LLMs to access external resources like Bing search or other tools. While innovative, it didn't take off. Anthropic, however, seems to have approached this differently.

Core MCP Features

MCP aims to solve the N×M integration problem between LLM applications and context providers by standardizing how LLMs interact with external tools and data sources.

In the community, the NxM problem is defined as having M different prompts for N different llms.

The protocol provides three main primitives:
- Tool calling for executing functions
- Resources for accessing read-only data
- Prompts for standardized interactions

 

 

There is a human in the loop because, at the moment, only Claude Desktop supports MCP, First step would be to go through the MCP documentation, starting with the Getting Started guide. or Glama.ai guide. I am familiar with using Claude via AWS Bedrock, so planned on integrating directly there, but, again, MCP is only supported on Claude desktop as of today, apparently, the idea will be to extend it, and soon be able to have generation and tool use contained within a customer’s AWS account using bedrock, but yet to see. Careful here!

 

Remains unknown if other LLM providers will adopt this tool protocol, at the time of this blog, there is one main alternative to the MCP for connecting AI models with external data and tools called UIM (Unified Intent Mediator) Protocol.

The key difference between MCP and UIM is while MCP focuses on providing a standardized protocol for two-way connections, the UIM Protocol takes a different approach by emphasizing intent-based interactions and ethical considerations in AI-service communications. It is quite complicated that protocol.

For enterprise teams considering MCP, the current iteration is best used as a prototyping tool. MCPs' success will depend on the adoption of the major industry players, like Google, Amazon, Microsoft, or OpenAI. 

I hope if this becomes mainstream, we could have our own artifacts built for our local and cloud services, so we could have these artifacts running on kubernetes, interacting with other cloud services and LLMs (Visionary mode)

Other important question I have since I got to it is (if they release it apart from Claude Desktop) choosing between building a tool using the MCP protocol or LangChain, where some of us have multiple tools already defined.

MCP is more flexible and lies in a lower level than LangChain. MCP might be better if you want more control over the integration layer and prefer to align closely with open standards rather than relying on a library like LangChain.

But, LangChain provides out-of-the-box connectors to various data sources (databases, APIs, document loaders) and it allows quick experimentation, it is not tied to the underlying infrastructure. The protocol currently is limited to the protocol where the same Client and Server is located, in the current case, a Desktop.

Also, LangChain offers a thriving community, and many utilities for memory, chaining prompts, and reasoning. Its not for the developer to be managing protocols and standards, but makes the development suitable only for the library which is built for.

But, for large enterprise vendors, such as SAP, a protocol is what is needed and here is where I believe that the original intent of OpenAI was, to provide open standards and protocols, so kudos to Anthropic I hope it takes off.
