entry.title: SAP ABAP SDK for IBM watsonx v1.2.0 released
entry.content:
On March 28, 2024, IBM had published the first release of its ABAP® Software Development Kit (SDK) for IBM® watsonx™ services. The watsonx.ai service can be used to train, validate, tune, and deploy foundation and machine learning models. The watsonx.ai Runtime, formerly known as Watson Machine Learning, is used to build, run and manage generative AI and machine learning applications with built-in performance and scalability.

The ABAP SDK provides a class library that can be downloaded to SAP systems using abapGit and comes in two flavors:

  1. ABAP SDK for IBM watsonx, using SAP NetWeaver for the SAP Business Suite running on SAP NetWeaver 7.50 or above and for the SAP S/4HANA on-premises edition.
  2. ABAP SDK for IBM watsonx, using SAP BTP ABAP Environment for the SAP Business Technology Platform ABAP Environment.

On December 3, 2024, version 1.2.0 of the SDK was released by IBM. Enhancements in this release include new watsonx.ai methods in the groups Text Chat, Text Extraction and Text Rerank. More information about the changes in version 1.2.0 can be found in the repositories in the migration document MIGRATION-V1.2.0.md.

Detailed information on how to setup and use the SDK can be found in the README.md files of the GitHub repositories. The README.md files also provide two comprehensive examples to show how the supported services can be used. An even more elaborated article about how use watsonx services with the ABAP SDK can be found in Joachim Rese's blog article How to Bring Generative AI to Your ABAP Program Using the ABAP SDK for IBM watsonx.

entry.title: AI-Driven Intelligence for Applications Built on SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Today’s application users face an overwhelming challenge of making critical business decisions while trying to navigate an unprecedented surge in data. For developers, this translates to building applications that can not only process larger volumes of data but also manage the complexity of data across diverse formats from a myriad of sources—structured or unstructured, local or remote, and everything in between. Yet, many development tools often fall short in meeting these demands. Legacy applications were not designed to handle the scale and complexity of today's information or deliver the intuitive experiences application users now expect. At the same time, developers face increasing pressure to incorporate even more artificial intelligence (AI) into their solutions.

Intelligent data applications (IDAs) address these three significant trends: ubiquitous data, natural human interaction, and intelligent processing. SAP HANA Cloud especially powers these next-generation applications with advanced database features like machine learning (ML), generative AI, and sophisticated analytics. With its powerful combination of speed, flexibility, and innovation, SAP HANA Cloud’s in-memory database is the foundation for building applications that help businesses realize the full potential of their data. These intelligent applications not only surpass traditional tools but are specifically designed to enhance the experiences of application users and optimize their roles within the company’s business processes.


The Database for Next-Generation Applications 

Using SAP HANA Cloud, developers can create applications that transcend traditional data processing, incorporating data science technologies like machine learning to continuously learn and adapt. By leveraging these capabilities, developers enable intelligent data applications to enhance user experiences, elevate application user expertise, and streamline business operations.  

Four core capabilities make SAP HANA Cloud the database for building IDAs: 

1. Data Integration: SAP HANA Cloud connects to a wide variety of data sources, including both SAP systems and third-party platforms. This solution offers flexible methods for the application to federate and/or directly integrate all types of business data into the database.

2. Multi-Tiered Storage: With options for storing data in-memory (hot), on disk (warm), and/or in a data lake (cold), SAP HANA Cloud ensures businesses can optimally store data balancing access speed, data density, and cost efficiency.

3. Multi-Model Processing: Advanced processing engines within SAP HANA Cloud bring together diverse data types—relational, graph, spatial, vector, and more—into a single database, eliminating the complexity of data-rich applications that must access multiple niche solutions.

4. Advanced Analytics & AI: Embedded machine learning libraries empower sophisticated business applications with predictive models, advanced data analysis, and forecasting capabilities.

Together, these four features of the SAP HANA Cloud database enable tailored solutions that empower application users to perform at a higher level, augmenting their expertise beyond what their years of experience alone would allow. Ultimately, intelligent data apps on SAP HANA Cloud pave the way for delivering enhanced experiences that users desire, while driving greater effectiveness within the company’s business operations.  

Note: For those interested in learning more about intelligent data applications, check out our new e-book, Intelligent Data Apps: The Impact of Generative AI on Applications. 


Machine Learning Pushdown to the Database 

Machine learning is a cornerstone of intelligent data applications and modern business innovation. With the rise of trends like generative AI, users now expect intuitive and intelligent interactions with their applications. As a result, businesses are increasingly turning to AI to meet the demand for these enhanced, personalized experiences. To help developers and data scientists deliver such applications, SAP HANA Cloud offers an integrated environment where machine learning and other AI capabilities are embedded within the database and can be directly called from the application layer of the stack. 

One of the key advantages of having machine learning embedded within SAP HANA Cloud is that it eliminates the need for complex integrations with additional ML and AI runtimes and environments. In turn, businesses can streamline data analysis, leverage real-time insights for better decision-making, and deliver innovative solutions even faster. In summary, embedded machine learning offers two major benefits:

  1. Optimal design: Algorithms run where the data resides, eliminating costly and time-intensive data transfers.

  2. Singular data model: A unified data architecture reduces the complexity of deploying and managing machine learning workflows. 

Additionally, SAP HANA Cloud makes it easier to automate and simplify the creation of machine learning models using tools like AutoML (automated machine learning). These capabilities enable developers with limited data science experience to incorporate the right ML models into their solutions. For data science experts, on the other hand, the flexibility and depth of SAP HANA Cloud’s machine learning capabilities enables even more sophisticated analyses. 


Machine Learning Libraries in SAP HANA Cloud 

SAP HANA Cloud offers two specialized machine learning libraries: the Predictive Analytics Library (PAL) and the Automated Predictive Library (APL). Each library is designed with distinct features to suit both scientific and business needs.


Predictive Analytics Library (PAL)  

The Predictive Analytics Library (PAL) is a robust toolkit tailored for data scientists and developers with deep mathematical understanding. Optimized for massively parallel in-memory processing, PAL provides over 100 advanced ML algorithms for scalable applications, including classification, regression, clustering, and time series forecasting. Its versatility and depth make it ideal for sophisticated use cases, from building intelligent models to leveraging specialized functions for advanced analytics. 

Common PAL use cases include:

  • Clustering: Segmenting customers for targeted marketing.
  • Time series forecasting: Predicting sales trends or inventory needs.
  • Outlier detection: Identifying fraud in financial transactions or anomalies in sensor data.
  • Text classification and search: Deriving the semantic context from text data stored in SAP HANA Cloud using text embedding models.


Automated Predictive Library (APL) 

The Automated Predictive Library (APL) simplifies machine learning for non-expert data scientists, business analysts, and developers, enabling them to utilize predictive modeling without extensive technical expertise. Designed with ease of use in mind, APL incorporates AutoML functionality to streamline the entire machine learning process. By automating tasks such as data preparation, variable encoding, and model testing, this ML library allows teams to quickly apply machine learning and focus on generating actionable insights rather than navigating technical complexities. 

Common APL use cases:

  • Customer retention: Predicting customer churn to inform retention strategies.
  • Sales forecasting: Using time series analysis to predict demand.


Get Hands-on with Machine Learning on SAP HANA Cloud 

Whether you’re a seasoned expert or just beginning your journey into the world of AI, SAP HANA Cloud provides a range of resources to explore and experiment with its machine learning tools:

1. SAP HANA Cloud Basic Trial – Try out SAP HANA Cloud’s machine learning capabilities, including the Predictive Analytics Library (PAL) AutoML features. Use sample data in a free, no-commitment trial environment to get started and explore what the platform has to offer.

2. SAP BTP Free Trial – As the primary choice for individuals and trainings, the SAP BTP free trial is a time-limited sandbox account that gives you a comprehensive, hands-on experience of SAP BTP, including services like SAP HANA Cloud.

3. SAP HANA Cloud Free Tier – As an enterprise customer, access free service plans for SAP BTP solutions (limitations apply), including SAP HANA Cloud. Follow this hands-on machine learning tutorial and easily upgrade to a paid version when you're ready—without losing any of your work.

4. Learning Journey: Developing Regression Models with the Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA – Follow a step-by-step guide to create machine learning workflows using the Python ML client for SAP HANA and the hana-ml library. To learn more, go through these additional ML tutorials, review the hana-ml overview, and check out some code examples on GitHub.

5. AI and Machine Learning Blogs – Stay informed with the latest updates to PAL (as of Q3 2024) or explore a comprehensive list of machine learning enhancements in SAP HANA Cloud since its release.

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What' s New in Q4 2024
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP is always evolving to better serve our customers. With the latest update in Q4 2024, in addition to quarterly content maintenance we have added new product pages.

We collaborated closely with SAP BTP experts to add three new product pages that consolidate links to all available release communication channels, like schedules, pre- and post-release communication, blogs, and more.

The new pages are:

  1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime
  2. SAP Mobile Services
  3. SAP Translation Hub

Read more about the details in this blog post.

 

1. SAP BTP, Kyma runtime

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, documentation on releases and available modules, the GitHub Kyma repositories, as well as a helpful learning journey and hands-on tutorials.

Check it out.

Fig 1. The new page for SAP BTP, Kyma runtime.

 

2. SAP Mobile Services

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out.

Fig 2. The new page for SAP Mobile Services.

 

3. SAP Translation Hub

This page contains links to release schedule information, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New information in the SAP Help Portal, links to the SAP Community, and helpful documentation.

Check it out.

Fig 3. The new page for SAP Translation Hub.

 

Currently, the release navigator for SAP BTP covers content for 30 products and services on dedicated pages. For all products and services not featured with their own page you can find helpful resources on the new General page.

Expect more new pages dedicated to specific products and services in future updates and don't hesitate to let us know via our feedback survey, which products or services you would like to see added next. 

 

Please note that the product page for SAP Data Intelligence Cloud has been retired as of this update. For more information, you can refer to this blog post.

 

For centralized information on when SAP BTP products and services release new features refer to the Consolidated Release Schedule for SAP BTP.

Regarding Release Navigator updates, you can refer to this update plan:

*Disclaimer:  The schedule is subject to change without prior notice including, when necessary, delays or incorporating changes in subsequent update windows and not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver any material, code, or functionality.

Looking ahead, 2025 updates will mainly focus on quarterly maintenance of release resources and a steady extension of product coverage. Currently, there are no plans for further mayor UI updates in the near future.

 

The Readiness@Scale team at SAP Enterprise Support is dedicated to ensuring release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to be prepared and ready to adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have any feedback or specific requests for content in the release navigator, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

 

And don't forget to check out our team's blogs tagged with Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale 
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: New podcast - SAP Learning Ins Artificial Intelligence Adoption with Thomas Jenewein
entry.content:
Dear Learners,

I am glad to inform you that we have recently been published on SAP Podcasts

SAP Learning Insights Artificial Intelligence Adoption with Thomas Jenewein

In this episode of SAP Learning Insights, David Chaviano welcomes Thomas Jenewein to discuss the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) within organizations. They explore the psychological aspects of AI implementation, emphasizing the importance of understanding diverse stakeholder perspectives for successful integration.
Thomas shares real-world examples demonstrating how AI can enhance productivity and creativity while addressing employee concerns about job security. The conversation also highlights the role of digital natives in driving AI change and innovative training methods for effective learning. David and Thomas advocate for a human-centered approach to AI, encouraging organizations to leverage it as a partner in innovation.

Tune in for key insights on navigating AI implementation in the workplace!

How to get started SAP Learning here are some resources:

  • Visit SAP Learning
  • Learn more about SAP Learning Hub
  • Blog "AI Adoption: from technology to successful usage, acceptance to adaption and transformation”

    Enjoy listening to this new podcast!

    Subscribe to via

    Spotify   Apple Podcast    TuneIn

entry.title: Document Information Extraction Free Tier Now Includes Premium Edition Capabilities
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce an exciting upgrade to the Free Tier service plan for Document Information Extraction! As of now, the Free Tier option on SAP BTP offers the advanced capabilities of the Premium Edition plan. This includes access to cutting-edge Generative AI features driven by the latest advancements in the space of Large Language Models (LLMs).

Enhanced Capabilities Now Available

The integration of the Premium Edition capabilities into the Free Tier plan means that you can now leverage all the powerful features such as:

- Auto Extraction without model training: Automatically extract business-relevant entities from documents without the need for extensive model training.

- Instant Learning Based on User Corrections: The platform now learns and improves in real-time based on user inputs and corrections.

- Embedded Document Classification: Classify documents seamlessly with embedded classification capabilities.

These features enable businesses to automate and accelerate document processing, improving efficiency and accuracy without additional costs.

Getting Started with the Enhanced Free Tier

To start using the upgraded Free Tier service plan for Document Information Extraction, follow these steps:

  1. Register and create an account in SAP BTP: This account will grant you access to the Document Information Extraction service with the Premium Edition capabilities.
  2. Explore the Free Tier Quota: The free tier offers a quota of 50 uploaded document pages per tenant within a rolling period of 30 days. Make the most of this allocation to test and build your scenarios.

Tutorials to Get Enabled

We have prepared tutorials to help you set up and utilize the Free Tier plan of the Document Information Extraction service:

- Get an Account on SAP BTP to Try Out Free Tier Service Plans: Step-by-step instructions to register and create your SAP BTP account.

- Use Free Tier to Set Up Account for Document Information Extraction and Go to Application: A guide to setting up the Document Information Extraction service and accessing the application.

These resources will empower you to fully test and explore the capabilities of the Premium Edition within the Free Tier plan. 

When you’re ready to take the next step, transitioning from the Free Tier to productive usage is seamless. To make this transition even more appealing, we’ve introduced a new pricing structure with multiple tiers. For more details about this updated pricing model, be sure to check out this article article.    

Conclusion

This upgrade is a testament to our commitment to providing high-value tools and services accessible to all businesses. By integrating Generative AI capabilities into the Free Tier plan, we aim to democratize access to advanced document processing technologies, enabling you to harness the full potential of SAP AI Services.

entry.title: Unlocking Value with New Tiers and Pricing for Document Information Extraction Premium Edition
entry.content:
We hope this message finds you well. We have some exciting updates to share regarding our service that are designed to enhance your experience and provide even greater value.

Price Adjustment and New Tiering Concept

As part of our ongoing commitment to delivering cost-effective solutions, we have introduced a new pricing structure along with a tiering concept for our premium edition of the service. This change is particularly beneficial for high-volume use cases, offering more attractive pricing options for our valued customers.

The new pricing structure applies to the service plan: premium_edition. The metric is blocks of 100 documents per month:

- Up to 5 blocks (500 documents)

- Up to 500 blocks (50,000 documents)

- Up to 1,000 blocks (100,000 documents)

- From 1,000 blocks onwards (100,000+ documents)

These new tiers make it easier for you to leverage the cutting-edge large language model technology in a secure and reliable manner, adhering to SAP product, security, and DPP standards at more favorable pricing. Please check the price updates in SAP Discovery Center.

We also want to inform you that as of this month you can also embark on your Premium journey with the 
Free Tier service plan. It allows you to test and explore the service free of charge. You can then easily switch to the productive environment once you're ready. For more details about the enhancements to the Free Tier plan, please refer to this article.

Key Values of Our Service

We'd also like to remind you of the key benefits our Document Information Extraction service offers:

- Auto Extraction without Model Training: Automatically extract business-relevant entities from documents without the need for extensive model training.

- Instant Learning Based on User Corrections: Our platform improves in real-time based on your inputs and corrections, ensuring that the service evolves to meet your specific needs.

- Embedded Document Classification: Experience seamless document classification with our embedded capabilities, making your workflow more efficient.

- Expanded Availability in Data Centers: our service is now available in additional data center regions to enhance accessibility and performance: Azure Netherlands (Amsterdam), Azure U.S. East (Virginia), Azure Australia (Sydney), AWS Southeast Asia (Singapore).

Note: further deployments will follow until the end of 2024: GCP Germany (Frankfurt), GCP U.S. Iowa (Central), GCP Japan East (Tokyo).

We are continuously working to improve our service offerings, and we appreciate your support and the continuous feedback that we are receiving from you.

Thank you for being a valued member of our community. Should you have any questions or require further information, please do not hesitate to reach out.

entry.title: Introducing the SAP Cloud SDK for AI (Java) 🎉
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce the initial release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI for Java! Only a few weeks ago we already announced the release of the JavaScript/TypeScript variant. Similarly, this SDK for Java enables convenient integration of generative AI capabilities within your SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) applications and allows you to utilize the Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core.

For SAP BTP developers, the AI SDK is crafted to simplify AI integration and enhance application impact. With features that optimize deployment, improve content safety, and facilitate model orchestration, the SDK lets you bring advanced AI functionality to your applications swiftly and with minimal setup. Whether you need adaptable workflows, secure data handling, or smooth generative model integration, the SDK equips you with robust tools to embed AI-powered features in your SAP BTP solutions.

This post introduces the main modules and their features.

 

AI Core - Setup and Usage

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.sap.ai.sdk</groupId>
  <artifactId>core</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>

Automate tasks such as creating AI Core artifacts, configurations, and deployments, executing batch inference jobs, as well as managing Docker registries and object storage for training data. The core module provides tools for workflow and scenario management within SAP AI Core.

  • Artifact management: register and organize datasets and model artifacts.
  • Configuration management: set up configurations for various models and use cases.
  • Deployment management: deploy AI models and manage their lifecycle within SAP AI Core.

Example SDK code: Create a deployment in SAP AI Core.

var api = new DeploymentApi();
var resourceGroupId = "default";
var request = AiDeploymentCreationRequest.create().configurationId("12345-123-123-123-123456abcdefg");

AiDeploymentCreationResponse deployment = api.create(resourceGroupId, request);
String id = deployment.getId();
AiExecutionStatus status = deployment.getStatus();
You can learn more about the SDK's capabilities for SAP AI Core in the public repository guide.
 

AI Core - Orchestration

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.sap.ai.sdk</groupId>
  <artifactId>orchestration</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>
This orchestration module lets you use the Generative AI Hub Orchestration Service with templating, content filtering, and data masking from within your applications. The Orchestration Service streamlines AI interactions while ensuring adherence to content safety guidelines.
  • Templating: Build dynamic prompts with placeholders to tailor AI interactions to user inputs.
  • Content Filtering: Apply filters to maintain compliance with content safety guidelines.
  • Data Masking: Anonymize and pseudonymize sensitive data.
  • Grounding: Add external data sources for contextually relevant information (planned for Q1 2025).

Example SDK code: Write a simple chat completion.

var client = new OrchestrationClient();
var config = new OrchestrationModuleConfig().withLlmConfig(OrchestrationAiModel.GPT_4O);
var prompt = new OrchestrationPrompt("Hello world! Why is this phrase so famous?");
var result = client.chatCompletion(prompt, config);

String messageResult = result.getContent();
You can learn more about the SDK's capabilities for Orchestration Service in the public repository guide.
 

AI Core - Foundation Models

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.sap.ai.sdk.foundationmodels</groupId>
  <artifactId>openai</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>
The openai module, along with other modules in the com.sap.ai.sdk.foundationmodels group, enables streamlined access to specific generative AI models available through the Generative AI Hub. The module provides a simplified interface focused on direct model interactions, ideal for developers who require direct access to foundation models for inference and embedding requests without additional orchestration features.

Currently only openai is supported. Please open a feature request, if you need direct LLM access for other foundation models.

Example SDK code: Write a simple chat completion.

var result = 
  OpenAiClient.forModel(GPT_35_TURBO)
    .withSystemPrompt("You are a helpful AI")
    .chatCompletion("Hello World! Why is this phrase so famous?");

String resultMessage = result.getContent();
You can learn more about the SDK's capabilities for foundation models and OpenAI specific features in the public repository guide.
 

Getting Started

You will need Java 17 or higher. Spring Boot or SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) as a framework is recommended, but not required. To explore these packages further, check out our sample project, which shows the usage of the various SDK packages.

 

Support and Feedback

We’d love your feedback on this first release! For support or to share your ideas, feel free to open an issue on GitHub.

 

Latest News

  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI for JavaScript released in October 2024.
  • Visit sap.com/ai and explore our portfolio
  • Explore the available AI capabilities on SAP Discovery Center.
  • Discover the latest announcements in the SAP TechEd Press Release and the SAP TechEd News Guide.
  • Review the SAP Road Map Explorer for a detailed view of upcoming product innovations.
  • Join the SAP Community page to connect with experts and share knowledge.

entry.title: Success factors for custom AI use cases: from the idea to the productive phase
entry.content:
The question of whether or not the use of AI in a company can be a value-added booster has long been answered - in fact, it is almost negligent not to consider the use of AI in one's company. Nevertheless, our experience shows that only a portion of the AI use case ideas considered for implementation ultimately makes it to the productive phase. At SAP, we understand that the introduction of AI use cases is a challenging journey into often unfamiliar territory. This makes it even more important to have an approach that provides clear orientation.

A lifecycle stage model for categorizing AI use cases is a critical tool for navigating the challenges of AI adoption and ensuring successful implementation. It offers several advantages: Many initiatives fail due to technical difficulties, unclear goals, or a lack of user acceptance. By applying a staged approach, resources can be allocated more effectively, risks identified early, and focus directed toward promising projects. This ensures that AI use cases are efficiently developed, from the initial idea to impactful deployment. Additionally, it serves as an excellent methodology for monitoring whether there are sufficient ideas and use cases in the early stages to meet company KPIs, such as achieving a specific number of go-live use cases each year.

These insights are based on experience within a successful AI program at a client and are specifically true for custom AI use cases. It’s important to note that “Embedded” or “Joule AI” use cases can also be classified within the same stage structure, but it might be possible to start with them directly at a later stage or skip certain stages in between.

Introducing the five stages lifecycle model - from exploration to roll-out
As illustrated in Fig. 1, the lifecycle stage model, consisting of five stages, can be divided into two phases. The early stages require relatively fewer resources and less technological investment and focus on evaluating whether a use case is viable for productive deployment. If these early stages are successful and a use case is considered beneficial, the mature stages focused on productive use can follow.

Figure 1: Five stages lifecycle model

Early stages: From the idea to the prototype
In the early stages of an AI use case, the focus is on defining the user group, clarifying the intended benefits, and identifying potential risks. In addition, consideration is given to its integration into end-to-end processes. This structured approach ensures a lean start with minimal use of resources and reduces the risk of unnecessary expenditure. The specific characteristics of each stage are described below:

1. Exploration
In the exploration phase, use cases are evaluated based on their potential benefits, effort, and risk. The primary task in this phase is to identify relevant and feasible AI use cases. However, this assessment is initially only a rough estimate, without a clear concept, an examination of the underlying data quality, and an analysis of the end-to-end process.

2. Low Fidelity Prototype
The focus here is on the concept. Workshops, such as design thinking, are used to define the target group, benefits and process integration. The aim is to assess the fundamental feasibility, viability and desirability. An essential component is to obtain a clear picture of the underlying data quality in order to identify potential obstacles at an early stage. Additionally, it provides an excellent opportunity to rethink and transform business processes through the use of AI.

3. Functional Prototype
Based on the findings, a functional prototype has been developed to evaluate its technical feasibility. Furthermore, the AI model must be trained and improved, which is usually a key challenge. During the technical implementation, however, the focus is less on perfecting of the prototype but more on validating the concept of the LoFi prototype. This step ensures efficient resource management and prepares the transition to a possible, more resource-intensive productive use.

From validation to scaling: the path to productive use
If the early stages lead to the conclusion of a positive use case, the mature stages follow, which are characterized by productive use. This refers to use in day-to-day business. How these two phases of productive use differ is described as follows:  

4. Pilot
The aim here is to achieve productive use, which requires greater effort, perfection and technical robustness. Additionally, process integration and change management represent the key challenges in this phase. However, meaningful further development in these phases only takes place if the benefits of the use case have been sufficiently demonstrated in the previous phases (e.g. through the functional prototype).

5. Roll-out
The roll-out throughout the company takes place as soon as the use case has been successfully tested in the previous phases and confirmed to be useful for productive use, with a continued focus on change management and ensuring a consumer-ready AI. In order to avoid disruptions in day-to-day business, the use case should have been prepared in a stable and reliable manner.

This maturity level structure described above provides many advantages for the stability of your AI use case. You will have identified potential obstacles at an early stage. You will have ensured efficient resource management and you will be prepared to transition to a possible, more resource-intensive productive use. The functional prototype sufficiently demonstrates its use, and when productive, you can be confident that disruptions will be avoided in day-to-day business.

Shaping Your entire AI Program with the Lifecycle Stage Structure
In addition to the already mentioned advantages of a maturity level structure, this structure can provide another important strategic benefit for companies with ambitious AI goals that are developing several AI use cases in parallel.

Being able to observe the lifecycle during the stages enables systematic and successful further development of the entire portfolio.[1] For example, with an overview of the number of use cases in each phase, important strategic AI KPIs - such as the introduction of a certain number of productive use cases each year - can be realistically evaluated. This provides a fantastic overview of whether there are enough use cases in the early-stage pipeline to achieve the company's goal of bringing a certain number of use cases into productive use, considering that only a portion of use cases progress from idea to productive use. If necessary, targeted actions such as exploration workshops to come up with new use cases can be initiated to unlock new potential.

To significantly increase the number of AI use cases that go live, organizations must also prioritize certain critical factors. Success at this level requires more than just structure and ambition - it requires a focus on the fundamental phases of the AI use case lifecycle. In particular, the first three phases - exploration, low-fidelity prototype (LoFi) and functional prototype - are critical to future success. These early phases lay the foundation for overcoming challenges and ensure that the use cases can be deployed smoothly.

The following key success factors, derived from practical examples of custom AI use cases, highlight what organizations must focus on to turn their ambitions into reality:

  • Data understanding and data quality: A solid database and data knowledge are essential for predicting potential difficulties when designing implementation options.
  • Early error culture and agile project management: Trial-and-error approaches help to identify weaknesses at an early stage. Weekly planning and interim goals create flexibility and a clear overview. Agile development should be used especially from the creation of a functional prototype onward in order to enable early testing and react more quickly to new or changing findings.
  • Design thinking: The use of the design thinking methodology when creating a low-fidelity prototype enables user-centered development that identifies weaknesses at an early stage. It also promotes interdisciplinary collaboration and thus ensures creative solutions that simultaneously question the current process and streamline it where necessary.
  • Business involvement: Involving the business at an early stage provides realistic insights and improves subsequent acceptance. Especially when using design thinking, it helps enormously in the creation of personas or the as-is analysis to carry out a well-founded assessment.
  • Standardized assets and test data: Templates for carrying out the methodology and early prepared test data are real accelerators, as they enable the prototype phase to be completed more quickly through smart preparation and follow-up work from the workshops and prevent delays during testing
  • Clear leadership and stakeholder communication: Consistent communication and coordinated project management promote transparency and acceptance, especially in cooperation projects with partners who provide for instance, AI solutions for the respective use case.

Your journey to success
A lifecycle stage structure for AI use cases ensures transparency, efficiency and optimized resource management. It not only helps to bring projects successfully to the productive phase, but also to identify risks and challenges at portfolio level at an early stage. This paves the way for the sustainable and scalable use of AI.

The AI race is already well underway. Don’t miss the chance to approach this transformative topic in a structured manner and unlock the opportunities AI has to offer. Leverage the framework and recommendations outlined in this article to enhance your current or future AI use cases. If you’re finding it challenging to get started or feel stuck along the way, SAP Advisory Services is here to support you. Whether it’s through our use case exploration workshops or our service to develop a tailored AI strategy, we’ll gladly guide you on this exciting journey.

 

[1] AI Strategy Approach | Dr. Norbert Enge | SAP Business Transformation & Architecture

entry.title: SAPUI5 Meets TypeScript: A Smooth Migration Journey with Joule
entry.content:
As announced in March in this blog post by @OliverGraeff, the UI5 team has taken its first glance into the world of generative AI as a contribution to Joule in SAP Build Code. This blog post is dedicated to providing insights into the latest feature that offers AI-driven migration of UI5 applications to TypeScript.

TypeScript and UI5 – how can I use it ?

Since 2023, UI5 applications can officially be implemented in TypeScript by generating projects configured for transpiling of TypeScript or by migrating the project structure as described in this blog post by @AndreasKunz.

In addition to converting the JavaScript code to TypeScript, migrating UI5 projects to use TypeScript requires installing the required dependencies to the project and to enhance the build and server configuration with the ‘ui5-tooling-transpile-task’ and ‘ui5-tooling-transpile-middleware’.

Joule now provides AI-driven convenience functionality to migrate existing UI5 applications according to the latest recommendations on how to enable TypeScript.

Let’s migrate to TypeScript with Joule!

When using Joule in SAP Business Application Studio, there is now an additional /ui5 prefixed slash command /ui5-typescript for UI5 applications available.

While the standard /ui5 command can be used for general queries regarding UI5 development, /ui5-typescript will specifically provide options meant to migrate existing JavaScript-based UI5 applications to TypeScript. As soon as the command is selected, a list of suggestions will appear.

Migrating the project setup

If you  begin to write controller code in TypeScript, you need to migrate the existing project first to enable the required tooling.

 

What’s happening here? After sending the prompt, Joule will recognize the project structure of the application and adjust the dependencies in the package.json and configuration in the ui5.yaml. A click on the files displayed in the list will show the changes made by Joule and AI, here, for example, the changes made for package.json:

In addition to using AI, Joule will check the npm registry to retrieve the latest stable releases for the required dependencies, therefore the responses are a hybrid of AI and tooling-driven migration. The results should nevertheless always be double-checked before accepting them. For example, as shown in this screenshot, AI recommends doing a major upgrade of the UI5 CLI, which should be evaluated and tested.

The “Accept” button in the file list can be used to accept all file changes at once. This will also include necessary files, such as the newly added tsconfig.json, to configure your migrated UI5 project.

Migrating controller files

After successfully migrating the project, we recommend to migrate smaller batches of controllers to TypeScript. Again, select the /ui5-typescript command in Joule – the suggestions will provide the currently selected controller file in your project that will be migrated to TypeScript. The results can again be checked by clicking on the resulting files to view the differences.

As soon as the “Accept” button is pressed, the previous *.controller.js file will be deleted and replaced with the accepted *.controller.ts file.

You can also directly use /ui5-typescript and specifically ask for certain JavaScript files to be migrated. The recommendation is to migrate files in your application step by step to get the best possible results.

Still eager to learn more about UI5 and AI? These blog posts might be interesting for you!

  • Now AI Helps You Developing SAPUI5 Applications
  • SAP Fiori tools 2403 introduces Gen AI capability and more 
  • UI5 freestyle development in SAP Build Code with Joule

entry.title: 【SAP Business AI】無料のおすすめ学習コンテンツをまとめました。
entry.content:
皆さん、こんにちは。

SAP Business AIについてキャッチアップをしたいけどなかなか情報が取得できない・・・。
理解したいがどれから始めたらいいかわからない・・・。
という方も多いはず。

こちらの記事では、SAP Business AIに関する認定や無料コース、サンプルプロジェクト、再利用可能なサンプルコードについて知りたい方のために、活用できそうな学習コースをまとめました。特にカスタムAI開発の勉強に活用できるコースを中心にまとめています。

 

なお、全て英語のコンテンツではありますが、ブラウザの翻訳機能を活用すれば日本語で理解することも可能です。数年前に比べてブラウザの翻訳機能もだいぶ精度が向上しましたね。

ぜひAI機能の恩恵を受けつつ、スキルを向上させていきましょう！

SAP TechEd Japan 2024 リプレイ

2025年1月31日(金)までリプレイ配信が予定されています。
SAP TechED Japan 2024のBusiness AIトラック、およびSAPテクノロジートラックの内容は
それぞれ15分~30分でわかりやすくまとまっていますので、とてもおすすめです。

https://gateway.on24.com/wcc/eh/2650067/sap-teched-japan-2024?partnerref=1

 

SAP AI認定

現在、SAPが提供する無料のコースが1つあり、デジタルバッジとして達成記録を取得できます。

Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio
SAPのBusiness AI戦略、その原則、倫理、プライバシー基準、およびビジネスに提供する機会について学べます。

AIおよびSAP Business AIに関する無料コース

Learning how to use the SAP AI Core service on SAP Business Technology Platform
SAP AI Coreの機能の活用方法を概要から学べます。

Generative AI at SAP
ビジネスコンテキストでのAIの基本的な使用法と利点を理解するのに役立ちます。
AIの種類と価値を理解し、SAP製品およびソリューションでAI機能をより効果的に活用できるようになります。

Navigating Large Language Models fundamentals and techniques for your use case
Large Language Models（LLMs）の説明と、LLMsに対するSAPのBusiness AI戦略、およびユースケースに最適なLLMパフォーマンスのための技術と方法を提供します。

SAP Discovery Mission 

SAPでは、チュートリアルだけでなく、リファレンスアーキテクチャ、ステップバイステップの勉強内容、リソース、ベストプラクティス、サポートの詳細などを含むSAP Discovery Missionを提供しています。

以下はAI関連のミッションのリストです。長いものも多いですが、全体像が掴めますので、ぜひ年末年始に取り組んでみていただくことをお勧めいたします。

Get Started with SAP Business AI
SAP Business AIのオンボーディング方法をガイドします。

Get Started with SAP Build Code and Joule using Generative AI (Trial and Enterprise Account)
SAP Build Codeのセットアップ方法と、Jouleおよび生成AIを使用して最初のフルスタックアプリを作成する方法をガイドします。

Set Up SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub
SAP AI Core Generative AI Hubのセットアップ方法をガイドします。

Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors
SAP SuccessFactorsインスタンスでSAP Jouleをセットアップする方法をガイドします。

Harnessing Generative AI Capabilities with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine

SAP HANA Cloud、SAP Generative AI Hub、Azure OpenAI、およびLangchain Frameworkの機能を活用する方法をガイドします。

Get Started with Document Information Extraction and Generative AI
生成AIがLLMs（Large Language Models）を使用してビジネス文書処理のユースケースを自動化する方法を学べます。

Digitizing Criticality Assessments using SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad
現在製品化されている実際の顧客ユースケースに基づいた機能を実装するステップがわかります。

Develop a CAP-based (multitenant) application using GenAI and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)
CAPを使用してマルチテナントアプリケーションを構築し、Generative AI HubおよびHANAベクトルエンジンを活用してRAGを実装する方法をガイドします。

リファレンスアーキテクチャ

Retrieval Augmented Generation and Generative AI on SAP BTP
SAP AI CoreのGenerative AI Hubを使用して、さまざまなLarge Language Models（LLMs）をシームレスに組み合わせる方法を説明します。

Federated Machine Learning
SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodologyで定義されたユースケースカテゴリ「Predictive Analytics」のユースケースパターン「Federated Machine Learning」に関連しています。

SAP Business AIのサンプルコード

Social Media Citizen Reporting using Generative AI Hub

Embedding business context with the SAP HANA Cloud, Vector Engine

Bringing Open-sourced LLMs into SAP AI Core

Generative AI-based code development with Joule in SAP Build Code

その他Business AI関連情報

SAP AI Community

SAP TechEd Group

SAP Learning Journeys

 

気に入ったら、ぜひ「いいね！」をお願いします！

このブログが皆さんのお役に立てば幸いです。

entry.title: SAP Business AI : what does it cost?
entry.content:
In this article I want to discuss some aspects regarding the cost of embedded SAP Business AI that I think is especially important for SAP resellers / channel partners.

Pure cost is of course not the whole story and I will address three topics 1) cost, 2) business value and 3) “small letters”.

Lets look at a specific example:  Leveraging SAP Business AI to “write a case overview”.  I showed a demo of this functionality in this LinkedIn post.

 

Costs:  0.049 Euro.
This AI feature of “writing a case overview” is a premium embedded use case.  That means that the customer first needs to purchase SAP AI units before they can activate and start using the AI feature.  

When you do all the math, running the AI feature ONE time will cost 0.049 Euro. 

Background for calculation: in the cost estimator on the Discovery Center, we can look up that it costs 7 AI units (49 Euro) to run this specific AI use case 1000 times.  Thus, per use the cost is 0.049 Euro.

Value: ROI of 127551%
The value is obviously different per customer.  But it might be interesting to do a quick “back of the envelope” calculation.  Personally, I started my early career as a support consultant for an ERP vendor and often had to make summary of cases I worked on, reviewing all communication about a support tickets, customer licenses, product documentation etc.   I think it would have taken me at least 15 mins to put together a mail like shown in the demo.  Assuming a service agent costs 25 euro per hour, the savings are 6.25 Euro.   A pretty good ROI of 127551% !!!

Let’s extend the case:  If the service agent leverages the AI feature once a day (200 times in the year), the savings per year will be 1250 euro at a cost of 9.80 euro.  If the company has 20 agents doing the same, the cost would be 196 euro a year with a saving of 25.000 Euro. 

And there might be a lot of additional benefits, like processing customer cases faster, employee satisfaction, increased capacity of handle business growth without the need to recruit more people etc.

The small letters: really good news!
I think there are three important aspects for channel partners to understand:

Minimum purchase requirement:  The minimum purchase requirement for a customer is 100 SAP AI units, at an SAP list price of 700 euro (7 euro per unit).   Thus, for 700 euro a year, a customer can get going with embedded SAP business AI.    In the example above the company having 20 service agents using the “case summary” AI feature 200 times each a year will spend 28 SAP AI units for this.  The minimum they must purchase is 100 SAP AI units.  Thus, they will have 72 SAP AI units to run other SAP AI features. All of this for 700 euro per year.

Minimum usage requirements: In the example above, it might look like 1000 is the minimum usage requirement for this specific AI feature, but that is not what it means.  The 1000 is more a calculation example.  When we look at the SAP AI service list, we can see that there are “NONE” minimum usage requirements for this specific AI feature (see table below).  The same is true for the vast majority of SAP embedded AI scenarios.   Thus, if a customer wants to run the AI feature only one time, they can do this and have a lot of SAP AI units left to spend on other scenarios.  Also important to note that the AI units can be used across all the SAP solutions the customer runs, offering yet another level of flexibility.

SAP AI service list:

 

Expiration of SAP AI units: the entitlement / usage right of purchased AI units expires after one year.  So, it is important to plan properly and try to use all the credits before they expire.  They will automatically review for the next year.   It reminds me of the way it works with mobile service providers: you buy a bundle of Gb internet capacity and pay for it whether you use it or not.  The different is that these bundles typically expire every month while SAP AI units expire after one year.   If we really think about it, it is pretty similar for the SAP cloud user subscriptions: if a customer don't use them during the year, they too will "expire".   

Conclusion: very low hurdle for customers to get going.

In my opinion, the commercials around SAP embedded AI can work very well for SAP resellers focusing on midmarket: customers can get going for 700 euro a year with a lot of flexibility on how to use the purchased SAP AI units. 

In addition, the activation of SAP embedded AI feature is very simple.  It is mainly a user permission topic.  After all, the embedded AI features are “embedded” in the SAP solutions the customer is running.  That is why we talk about “activation “of embedded AI features, we do not talk about installation or configuration.    Note: It is another situation for Joule.  Joule runs separately (on SAP BTP).  For this reason, Joule does not fall under “embedded SAP business AI”.   Joule is a separate AI category itself.

Finally, for the end user it is very easy to start using embedded SAP AI features. The user runs a small wizard, the AI tool generates text and the user decide to accept the outcome or not.   Just watch the demo mentioned in the begin of this article to get a feeling for how it works.

entry.title: GenAI as an accelerator for innovation and sustainable revenue growth in Manufacturing
entry.content:
While AI alone won’t solve all business challenges in the  manufacturing industry, it has the potential to significantly enhance profitability and drive operational efficiency, allowing manufacturers to reduce costs, optimize resource allocation, and make better data-driven decisions. Its strength lies in its ability to navigate complex systems—ranging from production lines to supply chains to sales operations. Since these systems are closely interconnected, even minor adjustments in one area can lead to substantial changes elsewhere. By analyzing large datasets from diverse manufacturing operations, AI can reveal hidden patterns and inefficiencies that traditional automation might miss. This insight empowers manufacturers to optimize resource use, minimize non-value-added tasks, and adopt more sustainable practices, ultimately driving a profitable and sustainable future for the company.

 

AI’s capabilities can also empower businesses to monitor changes, predict outcomes, and enhance system performance in ways that were once unimaginable. For instance, consider GEnAI trained on extensive datasets of manufacturing variances. These systems can swiftly analyze millions of options to identify the best materials and suppliers for cost-effective product development—far more rapidly than traditional methods.

Additionally, AI-driven product configuration harnesses data from historical sales orders, supply chain forecasts, and IoT sensors to help manufacturers optimize resource and labor usage. This not only improves business performance but also reduces inefficiencies in the sales process by minimizing unknown variables through real time insights and detailed analysis of each sales opportunity for better decision making.

 

 

Let’s take a close look to some typical use cases.

In the Field Service Management area, GenAI has a significant impact on manufacturer service departments as it can deliver advanced knowledge to the technician to improve service execution or help automate repetitive activities. In field service GenAI can optimize inventory logistics including stocking and inventory location given when and where inventory may be required, which is a common challenge when a service order is set and task and parts need to be planned. In this case, AI can provide an indication of the best available part or best option for a part needed within a service order.

When the service call is completed, GenAI can automatically generate service documentation, reducing time from service to invoice and automating a highly manual process traditionally prone to human errors.

Another use case scenario is in supply chain for configurable product options forecast. In this case, manufacturers are facing supply chain disruptions more frequently that impact their operations and financial performance. Like all disruptions in the market, these are difficult to anticipate.

By leveraging GenAI, planners can act on external signals that impact the supply chain planning process, allowing manufacturers to significantly enhance their supply chain resilience. The ability to predict disruptions, analyze real-time data, and automate decision-making allows them to respond proactively to short-term shortages, minimizing the impact on operational continuity, costs and customer orders.

More specifically, in the Configure-to-Order scenarios the value of GenAI is significant. GenAI can analyze data from historical orders, external trends and sales orders to improve forecasting accuracy for components used to make customized products,  helping match supply and demand in the short term and making insight-driven decisions for the company medium-term business planning.

AI won’t single-handedly solve most urgent business problems in the industry, but it offers a powerful tool to scale and expedite strategic efforts, like in the case of AI capabilities for customer communications and follow ups. From efficient summarization of business data to faster collaboration with customers , AI enables businesses to measure and optimize resources in ways that traditional methods can't match.

While the technology’s possibilities continue to grow, companies need to re imagine entire domains and workflows rather than isolated use cases. Although there are a few use cases mentioned in this article,  the number of use cases possibilities is endless. Companies need to be selective about where to invest in AI because most of the use cases in large enterprises with resources to develop their own GenAI solutions reach a sufficient level of accuracy after several iterations of fine-tuning, model improvement or data labeling.

entry.title: Reimagining AI: Why Bigger Isn’t Always Smarter
entry.content:
In my last article, we explored how the scaling of AI is encountering significant roadblocks, with the law of diminishing returns coming into play. Despite adding more computing power and advanced algorithms, the "intelligence" of AI models is showing limited improvement.

Recently, I came across an insightful analysis by Ilya Sutskever, one of the co-founders of OpenAI, in a fascinating interview. His thoughts resonate deeply with the challenges we face today.

Key Takeaways from Sutskever’s Insights:

  1. Data as the New Fossil Fuel: Sutskever highlights a pressing issue—data is becoming a major bottleneck. The internet, once an abundant reservoir of training data, is now depleting its quality sources. He compares this to "fossil fuels," with good training data becoming increasingly scarce, as much of it has already been consumed.
  2. The Need to Look Beyond Pre-Training: He urges us to think beyond pre-training models and explore novel approaches like TTC (Train-Time Compression) or inference-time models. Synthetic data, while promising, has not yet succeeded in significantly enhancing model acceptance. Drawing a parallel to human evolution, Sutskever notes how smaller-brained early humans exhibited greater intelligence compared to larger-brained mammals like Neanderthals, emphasizing the importance of reasoning models. He discusses the emergence of reasoning-based models like O1, which we’ve previously explored, as the next frontier in AI.
  3. Rise of Autonomous Agents: Sutskever envisions a future where autonomous agents, designed for specific tasks, play a crucial role in advancing AI. These agents will possess self-reflection capabilities, enabling them to evaluate and improve their actions, ultimately paving the way for more self-aware and capable AI systems.

You can watch his analysis here.

A Macro View from Yuval Noah Harari:

Another must-watch discussion is Yuval Noah Harari’s thought-provoking interview on the role of AI in our future, where he delves into themes from his book, Nexus. Harari likens our current stage of AI development to the evolutionary leap from amoebas to dinosaurs—a process that spanned billions of years. However, in the case of AI, he believes this transformation could unfold in mere decades.

Harari emphasizes the profound implications AI could have on individuals, governments, and society at large. He advocates for a global dialogue, urging that everyone on Earth should have a voice in shaping the design and trajectory of AI, given its potential to impact every aspect of human life.

You can watch his interview here.

This thought-provoking discourse from Sutskever and Harari offers not just a glimpse of AI’s current challenges but also a hopeful roadmap for its future. It reminds us that innovation when guided by responsibility and collaboration, can shape a world where AI serves humanity in meaningful ways.

What are your thoughts on these ideas? Do you agree with the perspectives shared by Sutskever and Harari, or do you see other possibilities for AI's evolution? Share your opinions in the comments below—I’d love to hear from you!

If you enjoyed this blog and found it insightful, please share it with others. Together, we can spark a broader conversation about the future of AI and its impact on our world.

entry.title: Shaping the Future: SAP Analytics Cloud as a Resilient Force in the AI-Driven Evolution of Business
entry.content:
The Artificial Intelligence Storm

In today’s rapidly evolving technological world, businesses face the dual challenge of staying competitive while navigating through disruptive trends like artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML). Many platforms face the risk of obsolescence as they struggle to keep pace with changing demands. Nevertheless, despite these transformation trends, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) continues to prove its relevance by adapting and integrating into a dynamic ecosystem and landscapes of the companies and benefits from a continuous commitment to improvement and adoption. SAC remains a vital tool for organizations that are seeking to derive value from their data, as it is core to SAP’s product portfolio focusing on driving future innovation.

Evolving with the AI Revolution

SAC’s Integration and Privileges in the AI Storm

AI has fundamentally shifted the way organizations approach business intelligence (BI) and analytics. Traditional dashboards and manual reports are being enhanced or even fully replaced by predictive and augmented analytics and conversational BI tools. Companies are increasingly demanding platforms capable not only of analyzing historical data but also of anticipating trends and suggesting actionable insights in real time.

SAP Analytics Cloud is not just adapting to these demands but thrives to represent such an environment itself. It embeds machine learning and AI-driven analytics directly into its core functionalities and as a result, empowers its users to go beyond descriptive analytics. For example, its predictive planning capabilities allow businesses to simulate scenarios and to plan for outcomes with the highest accuracy. SAC supports other wide range of functionalities, such as "augmented analytics," which enables  AI to guide users through complex datasets and highlight patterns

Apart from the functionalities, one of SAP Analytics Cloud’s strongest assets is its seamless integration into SAP’s product ecosystem. Businesses that are already leveraging SAP solutions—be it SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, or SAP Ariba—find that SAC fits naturally into their workflows. This integration ensures a unified data landscape where users can access real-time insights without additional challenges or fragmented datasets.

SAC acts as a bridge between on-premises and cloud environments, allowing businesses to adopt hybrid analytics strategies. This flexibility is particularly critical for companies in industries with strict compliance requirements or those undergoing cloud transformations.

By remaining deeply embedded within the SAP portfolio, SAC reinforces its relevance. As organizations adopt new AI-driven tools and methodologies, SAC’s ability to integrate with emerging technologies ensures it evolves in parallel with the broader business environment.

Furthermore, SAP’s roadmap for SAC emphasizes agility, with regular updates and new features designed to align with emerging trends. This ensures that customers not only have access to state-of-the-art analytics but also a sense of security that their investment will remain relevant in the years to come. SAC is a dynamic platform, built to evolve and adjust in harmony with its users' needs.

Looking Ahead

The future of analytics is undeniably intertwined with Artificial Intelligence. Yet, the need for robust, reliable, and integrated BI tools remains unchanged. SAP Analytics Cloud exemplifies how a platform can adapt to technological shifts while remaining a dependable solution for businesses.

As the AI era progresses, SAC will continue to evolve, delivering actionable insights, fostering collaboration, and driving business outcomes. For organizations seeking a future-proof BI solution, SAC’s deep integration with SAP products and commitment to innovation makes it a natural choice

By focusing on integration, usability, and technological evolution, SAP Analytics Cloud is poised to remain at the forefront of analytics innovation, ensuring that businesses can thrive in an ever-changing world.

 

Andrii Pogorielov, Seda Agasarjan

Customer Services & Delivery MEE BT&A CFO Advisory
